name: Yoga Vasistha
shortDescription: A philosophical text in the form of a discourse by sage Vasistha to a young Rama, exploring themes of illusion, reality, and liberation.
description: The Yoga Vasistha (also known as Vasistha's Yoga) is a Hindu philosophical text traditionally attributed to the sage Valmiki, though its authorship is likely composite and developed over centuries. Presented as a discourse by the sage Vasistha to Prince Rama, it explores profound metaphysical questions concerning the nature of reality, illusion (maya), suffering, and liberation (moksha). Rama, disheartened by the perceived suffering of the world, seeks wisdom from Vasistha. The text utilizes stories, allegories, and dialogues to illustrate its philosophical points, emphasizing the power of consciousness and the illusory nature of the material world. It advocates for self-effort and discrimination as paths to overcoming suffering and attaining enlightenment. The Yoga Vasistha is considered a significant work within Advaita Vedanta, though it incorporates elements of Yoga and other Hindu philosophical schools.
subtitle: A Journey Through Illusion to Reality
author: Traditionally attributed to Valmiki, likely composite authorship
significance: A key text in Advaita Vedanta, offering practical guidance for spiritual liberation through self-inquiry and understanding the illusory nature of reality.

extendedContent.title: Origins & Mythology
extendedContent.content: The Yoga Vasistha is framed as a conversation between the sage Vasistha and Prince Rama. Rama, having returned from his travels, is filled with disillusionment and questions about the nature of existence. He seeks guidance from Vasistha, the family guru. Vasistha then embarks on a lengthy discourse, employing numerous stories and parables to illustrate the principles of Advaita Vedanta. The text presents a cosmology where the universe is a manifestation of consciousness, and the perceived reality is ultimately an illusion. The goal is to realize one's true nature as Brahman, the ultimate reality, and thus transcend suffering. The text does not adhere to a strict narrative but rather weaves philosophical concepts through a series of interconnected tales.

extendedContent.title: Role & Significance
extendedContent.content: The Yoga Vasistha plays a significant role in Hindu philosophy, particularly within Advaita Vedanta. It offers a practical approach to understanding complex metaphysical concepts, making them accessible through engaging narratives. The text emphasizes the importance of self-effort (purushartha) in achieving liberation. It suggests that through discrimination (viveka) and detachment (vairagya), one can overcome the illusion of the material world and realize their true Self. The Yoga Vasistha is studied and revered by spiritual seekers who are interested in understanding the nature of reality and attaining enlightenment. It provides a framework for living a more meaningful and fulfilling life by aligning oneself with the principles of Brahman.

extendedContent.title: Symbolism & Interpretation
extendedContent.content: The symbolism within the Yoga Vasistha is rich and multifaceted. The stories and characters often represent different aspects of the human psyche and the challenges encountered on the spiritual path. The concept of 'maya' or illusion is central, symbolizing the misperception of reality due to ignorance and attachment. The characters' journeys represent the individual's quest for self-realization. The landscapes and events within the stories can be interpreted as metaphors for the inner workings of the mind and the transformative power of knowledge. The ultimate goal is to transcend the limitations of the ego and realize the unity of all existence.

extendedContent.title: Legacy & Influence
extendedContent.content: The Yoga Vasistha continues to influence contemporary spiritual and philosophical thought. Its teachings on consciousness, illusion, and self-realization resonate with modern seekers. The text has been translated into numerous languages and is studied by scholars and practitioners worldwide. The emphasis on self-effort and practical wisdom appeals to those seeking a personalized approach to spirituality. The Yoga Vasistha's message of hope and the potential for transformation remains relevant in a world often characterized by uncertainty and suffering. Its influence can be seen in various contemporary spiritual movements and teachings that emphasize the power of the mind and the importance of self-discovery.

sources.author: Valmiki (attributed)
sources.description: The primary source for the teachings and stories of Yoga Vasistha.

sources.author: Swami Venkatesananda
sources.description: A condensed English translation and commentary on the Yoga Vasistha.

associations.name: Maya
associations.significance: Represents the illusion that veils the true nature of reality.

associations.name: Brahman
associations.significance: Represents the ultimate reality and the source of all existence.

associations.name: Moksha
associations.significance: Represents liberation from the cycle of birth and death.

cross_cultural_parallels.name: Plato's Allegory of the Cave
cross_cultural_parallels.similarity: Both explore the concept of illusion and the pursuit of true knowledge.

cross_cultural_parallels.name: Sunyata
cross_cultural_parallels.similarity: Emptiness or voidness, echoing the illusory nature of phenomena.

companions.name: Rama
companions.description: The prince who seeks wisdom from Vasistha.

companions.name: Vasistha
companions.description: The sage who imparts wisdom to Rama.

worshipPractices.name: Study and contemplation
worshipPractices.description: Reading, reflecting upon, and discussing the teachings of the Yoga Vasistha.

cultural.modernLegacy: The text's teachings are incorporated into modern yoga and meditation practices, influencing self-help and personal growth movements.
